rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations that reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings such as assignments in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Write output dependency contracts as guarantees relating visible outputs to visible inputs in type scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons unless the model provides an explicit data value
